evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Playbook claims to use @solana/web3.js v2 but the backend uses solders + raw JSON-RPC (not web3.js at all), and the frontend imports VersionedTransaction/sendAndConfirmTransaction from @solana/web3.js — these are v1 API surfaces, not v2's createSolanaRpc tree-shakable functions. Packages like @solana/react-hooks, @solana/client (with createClient/autoDiscover), @solana/kit appear fabricated/inconsistent with v2's actual API. Constraint explicitly required createSolanaRpc, which is absent.
evidence.executability: Frontend code has explicit placeholder comments ('This is a placeholder for client-side signing flow', 'In production, relay signed tx through RPC') — transaction is never actually signed or broadcast. Imports from likely non-existent packages (@solana/react-hooks, @solana/client, @solana/kit). Pydantic model declares amount_lamports as int but frontend sends string. Multiple non-trivial fixes required.
evidence.completeness: Has install commands, .env.example, backend code, frontend code, test command. Missing: no API key acquisition (Solana devnet is keyless — acceptable). However, the e2e example contains explicit placeholder TODOs/'...' style comments violating constraint (c). Env vars consistent between listed and referenced.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Cites solana.com/docs and github.com/solana-foundation repos (semi-official) inline near code via [n] markers, plus third-party blog. No fabricated URLs detected, but canonical docs.solana.com / solana-labs.github.io references are mixed with many GitHub source links.

rubric_notes: Playbook nominally targets web3.js v2 but never uses createSolanaRpc; backend bypasses web3.js entirely using solders, frontend uses v1-style imports. Contains explicit placeholder comments admitting incomplete signing flow, violating no-TODO constraint. docs_unavailable=true so long-tail handling applied — internal inconsistency (claims v2, uses v1 + fabricated package names) drives CORRECTNESS down.
